_description: JavaScript/TypeScript dangerous pattern catalog — Kiwi auto-detect + gap-fill

patterns.title: Hardcoded API Key in JavaScript
patterns.why: Hardcoded API keys in client-side code are exposed to all users

patterns.title: eval() Usage - Code Injection Risk
patterns.why: eval() executes arbitrary code and enables code injection attacks

patterns.title: innerHTML Without Sanitization - XSS Risk
patterns.why: Setting innerHTML with unsanitized content enables XSS attacks

patterns.title: Missing Error Handling for Async Calls
patterns.why: Unhandled promise rejections cause silent failures

patterns.title: console.log in Production Code
patterns.good: // Remove or use proper logging library
patterns.why: Console statements should be removed before production deployment

patterns.title: document.write() — XSS and Performance Risk
patterns.why: document.write() enables XSS and blocks page rendering

patterns.title: new Function() — Same as eval()
patterns.good: // Use safe alternatives to dynamic code execution
patterns.why: new Function() executes arbitrary code like eval()

patterns.title: postMessage Without Origin Check
patterns.why: postMessage without origin check accepts data from any origin — XSS risk

patterns.title: Sensitive Data in localStorage — XSS Accessible
patterns.good: // Use httpOnly cookies for auth tokens
patterns.why: localStorage is accessible to any JS on the page — XSS steals tokens

patterns.title: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML — XSS Risk
patterns.why: dangerouslySetInnerHTML renders raw HTML — must sanitize to prevent XSS

patterns.title: User-Controlled href/src — javascript: Protocol Risk
patterns.why: User-controlled URLs can use javascript: protocol for XSS

patterns.title: Hardcoded Secret in Client-Side Code
patterns.good: // Secrets must NEVER be in client-side code. Use server-side API.
patterns.why: Client-side secrets are visible to anyone inspecting the source

patterns.title: Open Redirect via window.location
patterns.good:
const url = new URL(params.get('redirect'), window.location.origin);
if (url.origin === window.location.origin) window.location = url;
patterns.why: Unvalidated redirects can be exploited for phishing

patterns.title: CORS Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * — Too Permissive
patterns.why: Wildcard CORS allows any website to make authenticated requests to your API

patterns.title: Object Merge Without Prototype Check — Pollution Risk
patterns.good:
const safe = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(req.body));
// or validate with schema first
patterns.why: Spreading/merging untrusted input can pollute Object.prototype via __proto__
